title: Bundle Of Joy

jokes.text: Y ou know, I used to think the only reason to kill a man was self-defense... but I just spent 45 minutes in the Volvo with another reason.
jokes.setup: Philosophical statement about justified killing
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's about being annoyed in a car
jokes.explanation: Strong opener with good misdirection from serious to petty complaint. Well-structured but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: This idiot is taking a mime class.
jokes.setup: Question about what's wrong with Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton taking mime class explains his behavior
jokes.explanation: Sets up Carlton's mime antics. Functional but predictable - mime classes are inherently silly.

jokes.text: Carlton performing mime actions while Will threatens him
jokes.setup: Carlton doing mime routine
jokes.punchline: Will's mounting frustration and threats
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that plays to Carlton's earnest character vs Will's street smart attitude. Visual beats enhance rewatch value.

jokes.text: A mime is a terrible thing to waste.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he didn't throw Carlton out
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'a mind is a terrible thing to waste'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts a well-known phrase. Economical and quotable, though pun-based humor has limited impact.

jokes.text: Some of us more than others.
jokes.setup: Explaining this is where they eat
jokes.punchline: Obvious dig at Uncle Phil's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Predictable fat joke about Phil. Economical delivery but relies on cheap physical humor stereotype.

jokes.text: And this is your mommy... and she look like she eating for a couple of brothers herself.
jokes.setup: Introducing pregnant Vivian to unborn baby
jokes.punchline: Joke about her pregnancy weight gain
jokes.explanation: Another predictable physical appearance joke. The 'couple of brothers' phrasing adds some character voice but it's still low-hanging fruit.

jokes.text: Y ou know, I was going to knit some booties or something... but you know, I had to get yarn... and needles and... I'd have had to learn how to knit.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his alternative to traditional pregnancy preparations
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of obstacles ending with the obvious one
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Will's lazy rationalization. Character-specific procrastination humor.

jokes.text: Will, I'm eating. - Y ou'll hear a lot of that. - Y ou're grounded.
jokes.setup: Phil complaining about the recording during eating
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate commentary confirming Phil's eating habits, Phil's quick punishment
jokes.explanation: Quick callback to eating joke with Phil's immediate authority response. Good rhythm and character consistency.

jokes.text: Okay, that about sums up Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil just grounded Will for the recording
jokes.punchline: Will's documentary-style analysis
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary that breaks fourth wall slightly. Economical and character-appropriate narrator voice.

jokes.text: Y ou're sick, man. See a therapist, okay?
jokes.setup: Will asking Carlton what sound represents his family position
jokes.punchline: Carlton's disgusted psychological diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Carlton's preppy psychological terminology as response to Will's crude question. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Care to elaborate? - A lot.
jokes.setup: Will asking Ashley to elaborate on her baby comment
jokes.punchline: Ashley's minimal, deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Economical and shows Ashley's attitude without exposition.

jokes.text: Don't feel bad. She just mad 'cause she found out she ain't adopted.
jokes.setup: Ashley being difficult about the baby
jokes.punchline: Will's cruel but funny explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts typical adoption jokes. Character-appropriate cruelty that gets a big laugh.

jokes.text: Bungee jumping.
jokes.setup: Vivian explaining they took precautions with Ashley like other kids
jokes.punchline: Ashley's deadpan dangerous activity suggestion
jokes.explanation: Absurd single-word punchline delivered deadpan. Economical and unexpected from young Ashley.

jokes.text: Hey, I move all the time. Let's have a party.
jokes.setup: Phil excited about baby moving
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous parallel logic
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic typical of Will's character. Not brilliant but fits his tendency to make everything about him.

jokes.text: What do I have to do, spit up on your shirt?
jokes.setup: Ashley feeling ignored as everyone fusses over baby talk
jokes.punchline: Reference to baby behaviors for attention
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about competing with a baby for attention. Character-appropriate jealousy with good specificity.

jokes.text: Y'all hear something? Y eah, how about me screaming at the top of my lungs?
jokes.setup: Ashley being ignored
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on being ignored
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about being ignored while actively being ignored. Good escalation and self-awareness.

jokes.text: That little girl who used to hang out around here. Pretty little thing. Black hair. - Amanda. Ashley. - Or was it Anna? - No. Ashley.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to remember Ashley
jokes.punchline: Escalating wrong names despite corrections
jokes.explanation: Classic escalating gag with good structure. Phil's obliviousness plays well, builds through repetition.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure it was Appalonia.
jokes.setup: Continued wrong name guessing
jokes.punchline: Most ridiculous name yet (Prince reference)
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation peak with pop culture reference. Absurd enough to be memorable while maintaining character.

jokes.text: Now, here come your two eldest siblings, Dopey and Greedy.
jokes.setup: Hilary and Carlton entering
jokes.punchline: Seven Dwarfs reference characterizing both
jokes.explanation: Clever characterization through fairy tale reference. Economical and captures both characters accurately.

jokes.text: No, man, for my money, classic musical comedy: Milli Vanilli.
jokes.setup: Carlton praising Brigadoon as classic musical comedy
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous counter-example
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference joke that shows class divide. Milli Vanilli was topical and absurd comparison to Brigadoon.

jokes.text: The whiner.
jokes.setup: Ashley wondering who she is now that she's not the baby
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical and brutally honest. Will's directness creates the humor through truth-telling.

jokes.text: Shut up, Carlton. - Good call.
jokes.setup: Carlton being selfish about inheritance
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate agreement with his own dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his self-awareness about being obnoxious delivered with his typical earnestness.

jokes.text: Like when you took me up to the attic window... and told me I could fly? Or when you told me it was okay to roller skate down the steps.
jokes.setup: Ashley looking forward to big sister activities with new baby
jokes.punchline: Dark examples of Hilary's dangerous 'help'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect escalation. Reveals Hilary's dangerous obliviousness through specific, memorable examples.

jokes.text: Judging by your lovely brothers and sisters... you're going to need an insurance policy before you're potty trained.
jokes.setup: Context of dangerous family dynamics
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry assessment of baby's survival odds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line - dry British wit with dark humor. Character-specific delivery and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Mom's 50 years old and you haven't pushed her out the door.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Hilary should move out at 30
jokes.punchline: Hilary's ridiculous comparison logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary logic - completely missing the point with characteristic self-centeredness and false equivalency.

jokes.text: Aren't you forgetting something, baby sister? - You're prettier than I'll ever be. - Something else.
jokes.setup: Hilary fishing for compliment
jokes.punchline: Ashley gives backhanded compliment, Hilary wants more
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Hilary's vanity and Ashley's reluctant compliance

jokes.text: So, tell me about your day at school... and move a little to the left.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking Ashley about her day
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's using Ashley as a human fan/shade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - feigning sisterly interest while being completely self-serving. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Y ou poor baby. Use the other arm.
jokes.setup: Ashley's arm hurting from holding the fan
jokes.punchline: Hilary's fake sympathy with selfish solution
jokes.explanation: Hilary's oblivious selfishness perfectly demonstrated. The fake concern followed by continued exploitation is character gold.

jokes.text: Oh, goody, a shoeshine kit.
jokes.setup: Hilary presenting Ashley with a 'surprise'
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of obvious sarcasm. Sets up the exploitation that follows.

jokes.text: Can I put the fan down first? - This time. It is your birthday.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking for basic courtesy
jokes.punchline: Hilary treating basic human decency as birthday generosity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Hilary's complete obliviousness to exploitation. The birthday caveat makes it even worse.

jokes.text: A spit polish. I meant on the shoes.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking for spit polish
jokes.punchline: Clarification that prevents misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke. The clarification suggests other interpretations, adding to Hilary's oblivious exploitation.

jokes.text: Maybe I'd just be better off being invisible. - I'm sorry, what'd you say, Ash?
jokes.setup: Ashley expressing feeling invisible
jokes.punchline: Will immediately proving her point by not hearing her
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - Ashley's complaint is immediately validated. Excellent setup/payoff with strong thematic resonance.

jokes.text: Y ou'll recognize him, he'll be the one at eye-level.
jokes.setup: Will introducing Carlton to the baby
jokes.punchline: Height joke about Carlton being baby-sized
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke about Carlton. Economical and character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Two birds! Two birds!
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating twins news
jokes.punchline: Carlton's excited but confused contribution
jokes.explanation: Carlton's characteristic attempt to join in with slightly wrong enthusiasm. Physical comedy implied.

jokes.text: I remember something about twins... and I have this incredible urge for a sausage.
jokes.setup: Phil recovering from fainting
jokes.punchline: Random craving mixed with confused memory
jokes.explanation: Absurd post-fainting confusion. The non-sequitur works for disoriented character moment.

jokes.text: We'll have to split the inheritance four ways... thanks to Forget-the-pill Phil.
jokes.setup: Twins announcement
jokes.punchline: Carlton's selfish calculation and nickname creation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - immediate financial calculation plus creative nickname. Character-specific greed with clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Now, you can finally have the son you always wanted.
jokes.setup: Will saying Phil can have the son he wanted, Carlton objecting
jokes.punchline: Will repeating the exact same line, dismissing Carlton
jokes.explanation: Brilliant repetition joke that completely dismisses Carlton's existence. The identical delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Preferably a young nanny... amply endowed, with no inhibitions.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting more help with triplets
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's inappropriate interjection with specific preferences
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry delivery of inappropriate suggestion. Character-appropriate subversion of formal butler persona.

jokes.text: An explanation. - Mid-life crisis.
jokes.setup: Vivian asking what Geoffrey wants
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's clinical self-diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Geoffrey's characteristic self-awareness. Economical and exactly what the character would say.

jokes.text: Geoffrey is so lazy.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey overwhelmed doing everyone's tasks
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious complaint while he's clearly overworked
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Hilary completely misreading the situation. Her obliviousness to Geoffrey's exhaustion is peak character.

jokes.text: The baby's.
jokes.setup: Asked when the parents will return from Jamaica
jokes.punchline: Implying they'll be gone until the baby graduates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant two-word punchline with perfect timing. Dark humor delivered with characteristic Geoffrey understatement.

jokes.text: Calgon, take me away!
jokes.setup: Geoffrey overwhelmed by family demands
jokes.punchline: Reference to bath product commercial
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Geoffrey's desperation. Not original but well-delivered and situationally appropriate.

jokes.text: He pretends he doesn't like the family... but deep down inside he really hates us.
jokes.setup: Will explaining Geoffrey pretends not to like the family
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by saying he really hates them
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts the expected 'but deep down he loves us' formula. Perfect structure and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Will, we're talking about kids, not malt liquor.
jokes.setup: Will wanting six kids one after another
jokes.punchline: Carlton's comparison to alcohol consumption patterns
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Will's approach to family planning. Carlton's preppy perspective on Will's street attitude.

jokes.text: Why don't you shut up before we send you back... to that white family that dropped you off on the doorstep?
jokes.setup: Carlton irritating Will
jokes.punchline: Threat referencing Carlton's adoption by wrong-race family
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Carlton's cultural disconnect. Plays on the racial dynamics of his character without being offensive.

jokes.text: Will's perfect family fantasy sequence
jokes.setup: Will describing his perfect family life
jokes.punchline: Fantasy sequence showing stereotypical perfect family
jokes.explanation: Visual sequence setting up the subversion that follows. Standard sitcom dream sequence but serves the larger joke.

jokes.text: Today we shot La Toya Jackson. And I had to keep turning the lights off 'cause she was melting.
jokes.setup: Fantasy wife asking about shooting naked centerfolds
jokes.punchline: La Toya Jackson melting under lights
jokes.explanation: Topical pop culture joke about plastic surgery. Absurd image that fits the fantasy format.

jokes.text: Hey, Shaquille, Barkley, Jordan.
jokes.setup: Will greeting his fantasy children
jokes.punchline: Kids named after basketball stars
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate naming choices for Will's fantasy kids. Shows his basketball obsession and aspirational thinking.

jokes.text: What are hoops?
jokes.setup: Will suggesting basketball with kids named after players
jokes.punchline: Kids don't know basketball despite their names
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - kids named after basketball players who don't know basketball. Subverts Will's expectations.

jokes.text: Y ou, you, you! Why must everything always be about you?
jokes.setup: Perfect wife offering everything Will wants
jokes.punchline: Will complaining about her focus on his needs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Will complaining that his perfect wife focuses on him too much. Shows impossibility of perfection.

jokes.text: I want a girl with extensions in her hair... and bamboo earrings, at least two pair.
jokes.setup: Will listing what he really wants
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural preferences with exact requirements
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's actual preferences vs. perfect family ideal. Specific details make it memorable.

jokes.text: Last time we sat around like this is when they reran Roots.
jokes.setup: Will noting rare family togetherness
jokes.punchline: Reference to when family watches serious programming together
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that rings true about family viewing habits. Specific and relatable observation.

jokes.text: Hey, it's a cruel world, big guy... and you're not going to be in it forever.
jokes.setup: Vivian saying they're not dead yet
jokes.punchline: Carlton's matter-of-fact mortality reminder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Carlton's characteristic matter-of-fact tone. The casual cruelty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Zip-a-dee, and might I add doo-dah!
jokes.setup: Being invited to dinner after being overwhelmed
jokes.punchline: Overly cheerful Disney reference with sarcastic addition
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's sarcastic cheer with Disney reference. The formal addition 'might I add' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Okay, but I'm not sitting between you and the dessert cart. Last time I almost lost an eye.
jokes.setup: Hilary agreeing to come to dinner
jokes.punchline: Specific violent imagery about Phil's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Specific physical comedy callback with vivid imagery. Hilary's self-preservation instinct about Phil's eating.

jokes.text: Why don't we just go eat out of a dumpster?
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Snuffy's Catfish and Biscuits
jokes.punchline: Hilary's snobbish comparison to dumpster diving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response showing her snobbery. Extreme comparison that's completely in character.

jokes.text: Where'd everybody go?
jokes.setup: Ashley finally feeling good about not being invisible
jokes.punchline: Everyone has literally left without her
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and visual punchline. The ultimate proof of Ashley's invisibility delivered at the perfect moment.

jokes.text: Geoffrey's rant about Will's sneakers and women
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's continued exhaustion
jokes.punchline: Stream-of-consciousness rant about Will's demands and personal life
jokes.explanation: Extended rant showing Geoffrey's breakdown. Less focused than earlier moments but shows character deterioration.

tmdb_overview: As Vivian's delivery date approaches, the family fantasizes about what the baby will mean to each of them: Ashley feels all but invisible, Geoffrey feels overwhelmed by the family's demands, and Vivian feels like she's having triplets.
